terms.definition: The argparse object you configure with add_argument and then call parse_args on to read the command line.
A required command-line value identified by its position rather than a flag name.
A flag-style argument (e.g. --model) that may be omitted, often with a default value.
The simple object returned by parse_args whose attributes hold the parsed argument values.
A nested parser created via add_subparsers that gives a subcommand its own set of arguments.
An argparse action that turns an option into a boolean flag, True when present and False (default) when absent.
